# agentic-velociraptor-mcp

> Secure-by-design Go MCP server for controlled Velociraptor DFIR operations: stdio-only, read-only by default, approval-gated for write/evidence operations, auditable, and intentionally closed-world.

## Project overview

`agentic-velociraptor-mcp` exposes a narrow, reviewed Velociraptor DFIR capability set to MCP-compatible agents. It is a Go server using the official MCP Go SDK, Velociraptor typed gRPC bindings, local DFIR profile/catalog files, policy gates, human approval records, and JSONL audit logging.

Current repository docs describe v1.0.0 as the first production release for strict controlled deployment. The callable MCP surface is intentionally fixed and tested: 28 registered tools covering visibility, DFIR profiles/workflow planning, flow/result access, controlled collection/download operations, hunt management, and a fixed-template IOC hunt helper.

## What this project is not

- Not a generic Velociraptor remote administration shell.
- Not a raw VQL or arbitrary query executor; the stable core has no `run_vql`/generic-query tool.
- Not a way for an LLM or MCP client to approve its own write/evidence request.
- Not a replacement for Velociraptor server-side ACLs; Velociraptor least-privilege identities remain the primary boundary.
- Not an HTTP/SSE network service in the current stable core; the server speaks MCP over stdio.
- Not a Velociraptor deployment manager, server installer, or SIEM/SOAR platform.

## Safety and security boundaries

Assume every agent/tool caller is untrusted and every endpoint result is evidence-like but not ground truth. Do not weaken these controls without explicit security review:

- Read-only is the default posture. Write/evidence operations require `policy.mode: controlled` and an approval store.
- Approval-gated tools require a human-created, fingerprint-matched, unconsumed approval from the separate `approve` CLI. No MCP tool can create or decide approvals.
- Every collection, hunt start/cancel, IOC hunt, flow cancel, and evidence download is allowlist- and approval-gated.
- Audit logging is expected for tool outcomes. Write-capable operations fail closed if the audit sink cannot record the attempt.
- Tool inventory is closed-world: only implemented/reviewed tools are registered with `mcp.AddTool`; tests pin the exact inventory and guard against unsafe tool names.
- Raw VQL is out of scope. Capabilities map to reviewed artifacts, DFIR profiles, fixed templates, and typed Velociraptor gRPC RPCs.
- Do not pass credentials through tool arguments. Velociraptor API configs are server-side files, loaded from configured paths, and must stay outside version control.
- Respect row, byte, upload, hunt-scope, artifact/profile, and target-all limits; do not silently broaden scope.
- Real-mode explicit `client_ids` hunt scope is unsupported by the typed Velociraptor RPCs and is refused before approval consumption; use label or all-clients scoping where policy permits.
- IOC hunting currently has cautious support: hash maps to `Generic.Detection.HashHunter`; `ip`, `domain`, `process`, and `path` fail closed until curated artifacts exist.

## Read first

- [README.md](README.md) — top-level status, quick start, design principles, repository layout, and documentation index.
- [PROJECT_STATE.md](PROJECT_STATE.md) — current milestone, known limitations, backend wiring status, and release history notes.
- [docs/release/v1.0.0-production-checklist.md](docs/release/v1.0.0-production-checklist.md) — required production deployment gate and accepted limitations.
- [docs/security-model.md](docs/security-model.md) — threat model, trust boundaries, no-raw-VQL rationale, MCP security practices.
- [docs/approval-flow.md](docs/approval-flow.md) — human approval lifecycle and fingerprinting model.
- [docs/tool-reference.md](docs/tool-reference.md) — current callable tool inventory and per-tool behavior.

## Important files and directories

- [cmd/agentic-velociraptor-mcp/](cmd/agentic-velociraptor-mcp/) — CLI entrypoint, stdio MCP server startup, and human-only `approve` subcommand.
- [internal/mcpserver/](internal/mcpserver/) — MCP server construction, tool registration, handlers, approval/audit integration, inventory tests.
- [internal/policy/](internal/policy/) — policy mode, allowlists, and routing decisions.
- [internal/approval/](internal/approval/) — approval store, one-shot consumption, and request fingerprinting.
- [internal/audit/](internal/audit/) — JSONL audit sink and secret redaction.
- [internal/validation/](internal/validation/) — syntactic validation for client IDs, flows, hunts, artifacts, IOCs, scopes, and request fields.
- [internal/velociraptor/](internal/velociraptor/) — typed Velociraptor gRPC client and domain interfaces.
- [internal/velociraptor/veloapi/](internal/velociraptor/veloapi/) — hand-scoped generated bindings for the RPCs this project permits.
- [internal/vql/](internal/vql/) — fixed IOC template binding only; not raw caller-supplied VQL execution.
- [internal/dfir/](internal/dfir/) — DFIR profile and artifact-catalog loading/validation.
- [catalog/artifacts.yaml](catalog/artifacts.yaml) — reviewed artifact catalog used by profiles and tests.
- [profiles/](profiles/) — curated DFIR profile YAML definitions.
- [examples/config/](examples/config/) and [examples/client-configs/](examples/client-configs/) — safe example configurations.
- [Dockerfile](Dockerfile) — distroless, non-root, stdio-oriented container build.
- [.github/workflows/ci.yml](.github/workflows/ci.yml) — canonical CI checks.

## Architecture map

High-level request flow:

1. MCP client launches the server over stdio and calls a typed tool.
2. `internal/mcpserver` validates request shape and routes to a narrow handler.
3. `internal/validation` and `internal/policy` enforce syntax, mode, allowlists, and scope limits.
4. Approval-gated handlers require a matching human approval before calling write-capable backend paths.
5. `internal/velociraptor` calls the configured typed gRPC RPC using least-privilege read/write identities.
6. `internal/audit` records exactly one outcome (`success`, `blocked`, or `error`) with redaction.

For detailed diagrams and package responsibilities, read [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md), but prefer [PROJECT_STATE.md](PROJECT_STATE.md) when older architecture prose conflicts with current implementation status.

## Verification commands

Use the repository's existing Go/CI checks; do not add new tooling for a docs-only change unless requested.

```sh
git diff --check
gofmt -l .
go vet ./...
go build -buildvcs=false ./...
go test ./...
```

For targeted safety checks, the existing tests include exact tool inventory and unsafe-tool guards in `internal/mcpserver`, safe example-config checks in `internal/config`, approval fingerprinting tests in `internal/approval`, and profile/catalog validation tests in `internal/dfir`.

## Contributor guidance for LLMs and coding agents

- Preserve runtime behavior, tool behavior, approval gates, audit behavior, permissions, release tags, and documented security posture unless the task explicitly says otherwise.
- Inspect docs and tests before changing code; several docs intentionally distinguish current implementation from historical milestones.
- If adding/changing a tool, update exact inventory tests, tool reference docs, security docs, and validation docs together.
- Do not invent Velociraptor RPC behavior. If a typed RPC path is absent or unvalidated, report it honestly and fail closed.
- Do not read, print, or commit real Velociraptor `api.config.yaml` contents, private keys, approval stores, audit logs, or evidence downloads.
- For documentation-only changes, prefer `git diff --check` plus available lightweight checks. Full Go tests are still safe and useful when dependencies are available.

- [docs/runbooks/controlled-pilot.md](docs/runbooks/controlled-pilot.md) — staged first-deployment procedure.
